mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 300 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 300.0 are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 70% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing and inventory accumulation, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal yields a negative predictive accuracy below 45% over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indices signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening economic output, driving negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO month-over-month changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of cases during recovery phases.
